title: ranunculus sulphureus var. altaicus
intro: Ranunculus sulphureus var. altaicus, also known as Altaic buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and deeply lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Altaic buttercup include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
